proof_notes: §5.3 two-proof bar (Phase 4 §4.1 lock-in). (a) Caller-context: parseSelectClausePrimary is invoked from parseSelectClause for every leaf of the set-op precedence climb. It routes on the leading token: '(' → parseSelectNoParens via left-factoring (the Phase 0 left-factoring means we never speculatively parse a select_with_parens at this site — we always parse select_no_parens and let the enclosing set-op producer decide), SELECT/VALUES/WITH/TABLE → simple_select directly. This matches PG's reduction where simple_select at the set-op leaf can itself begin with '(' through select_no_parens → simple_select recursion. (b) Empirical proof: paren_multi_join_test.go TestParseParenSetOpInSubquery (lines 100-118, 5 pinned scenarios — UNION/UNION ALL/INTERSECT/EXCEPT/nested set-ops all with parenthesized leaves) + TestParseParenMultiJoin (lines 23-55, indirect coverage through select_with_parens at FROM level) + TestParseParenSubqueryStillWorks (lines 145-155, 5 scenarios driving single/double/triple paren-wrapped leaves). Anti-regression fence: paren_oracle_from_subquery_test.go + pgregress `union.sql`, `select.sql`, `with.sql` cover the production set-op shapes end-to-end.

§5.3 two-proof bar (Phase 4 §4.1 lock-in). (a) Caller-context: parseSelectWithParens is the canonical entry point for the select_with_parens production (gram.y:12682 select_with_parens: '(' select_no_parens ')' | '(' select_with_parens ')'). It is reached from three dispatch sites — parseSelectClausePrimary (select.go:166), parseParenTableRef (select.go:1162 after parenBeginsSubquery scan), and parseLateralTableRef (select.go:1347 via LATERAL '(' arm). All three callers confirm '(' before delegating, so parseSelectWithParens's expect('(') is guaranteed to succeed; the left-factored body then delegates to parseSelectNoParens unconditionally, matching PG's grammar reduction (select_with_parens never fans out on any token past the leading paren). No disambiguation is possible or needed. (b) Empirical proof: paren_multi_join_test.go TestParseParenSetOpInSubquery (lines 100-118, 5 pinned scenarios: UNION, UNION ALL, INTERSECT, EXCEPT, and nested ((A UNION B) UNION C) — each inside a FROM '(' select_with_parens ')' arm) + TestParseParenSubqueryStillWorks (lines 145-155, 5 scenarios: single/double/triple paren-wrapped SELECT, VALUES, WITH CTE, TABLE forms) exercise select_with_parens through all reachable callers. Anti-regression fence: paren_oracle_from_subquery_test.go TestParenOracleFromSubquery + pgregress `union.sql`, `subselect.sql`, `with.sql` statements drive the nested-paren set-op shapes.

Conditional: checks p.cur.Type == '(' for optional paren-wrapped column list. Single-token peek sufficient because '(' always signals name_list form, else empty. PG grammar: common_table_expr → name opt_name_list AS... Exercised by pgregress `with.sql` (WITH cte(a,b,c) AS ... — column-list form) and `with_recursive.sql`; also covered by pgregress `insert.sql` INSERT ... WITH CTE cases.

Phase 1 §1.2: T6/T7 hybrid. T6 dispatch arms route each disjoint lead — '(' → parseSelectWithParens, ROWS → parseRowsFromTable (ROWS FROM sub-production of func_table), XMLTABLE → parseXmlTable, JSON_TABLE → parseJsonTable, default → parseFuncExprWindowless. T7 post-parse check rejects bare ColumnRef from the func_table arm because gram.y has no `LATERAL relation` production and the shared parser entry point for func_expr_windowless also parses plain identifiers. Caller-context: parseLateralTableRef is invoked only from parseTableRefPrimary after consuming the LATERAL keyword (select.go:1107). Pinned scenarios (paren_lateral_ref_test.go TestParenLateralRefDispatch at line 33): accept — LATERAL (SELECT ...) at line 39-40, LATERAL XMLTABLE at line 43, LATERAL JSON_TABLE at line 46, LATERAL f(t.x) at line 49, LATERAL f(t.x) WITH ORDINALITY at line 50, LATERAL f(t.x) AS fa(a int, b text) at line 51, LATERAL ROWS FROM at line 54-55, LATERAL CURRENT_USER (func_expr_common_subexpr) at line 61; reject — LATERAL (a JOIN b ON TRUE) at line 68, LATERAL u (bare relation) at line 74. Additional AST-shape proof: TestParenLateralRefSubqueryAST (line 94), TestParenLateralRefFuncTableAST (line 115), TestParenLateralRefRowsFromAST (line 135), TestParenLateralRefXmlTableAST (line 158), TestParenLateralRefJsonTableAST (line 175), TestParenLateralRefFuncTableAliasColdefAST (line 197, Alias.Aliasname + Coldeflist), TestParenLateralRefFuncTableOrdinalityAST (line 229, Ordinality flag).

After identifier consumed; '(' always signals func_table. PG grammar: relation_expr → qualified_name [opt_alias_clause], and separately func_table: func_name(...). Exercised by pgregress `rangefuncs.sql`, `functional_deps.sql`, `generated.sql` (function-in-FROM), and paren_oracle_from_simple_test.go TestParenOracleFromSimple.

After schema.attr; '(' always func_table. Precedent: parseColumnRefOrFuncCall uses same peek at expr.go:2452. Exercised by pgregress `rangefuncs.sql` (schema-qualified function-in-FROM), `window.sql`; same T1 peek pattern as expr.go:2452.

After catalog.schema.attr; '(' always func_table. Continuation of 2-part pattern. Exercised by pgregress `rangefuncs.sql` (catalog-schema-qualified func_table), continuation of the 2-part pattern; also qualified_type_multi_test.go Test* for qualified-name parsing.

§5.3 two-proof bar (Phase 4 §4.1 lock-in). (a) Caller-context: parseParenTableRef is reached only from parseTableRefPrimary's '(' arm for the joined_table vs select_with_parens fork (select.go parseTableRefPrimary). The parenBeginsSubquery T3 snapshot scanner classifies the paren content by looking past the leading '(' at depth-1 tokens: SELECT/VALUES/WITH/TABLE (or a following '(' that recursively satisfies the scan) → select_with_parens; otherwise → joined_table. Any content not matching joined_table is rejected (no silent bare-relation acceptance). (b) Empirical proof — ≥5 pinned tests spanning all dispatch arms: paren_multi_join_test.go TestParseParenMultiJoin (lines 23-55, 11 scenarios covering plain/aliased/column-list/double/triple nested joined_table, subquery-operand joined_table, and the BYT-9315 pg_get_viewdef shape), TestParseParenMultiJoinAST (lines 60-92, left-nested JoinExpr shape), TestParseParenSetOpInSubquery (lines 100-118, 5 scenarios select_with_parens arm), TestParseParenTableRefRejectsNonJoined (lines 125-143, 3 rejection scenarios for `(a)` / `((a))` / `((SELECT 1) x)`), TestParseParenSubqueryStillWorks (lines 145-155, 5 scenarios single/double/triple paren-wrapped SELECT + VALUES + WITH + TABLE). Anti-regression fence: paren_oracle_from_joined_test.go, paren_oracle_from_subquery_test.go, paren_oracle_from_degenerate_test.go, paren_oracle_from_mixed_test.go all drive PG 17 container oracle probes; pgregress `join.sql`, `subselect.sql`, and the full pgregress corpus run end-to-end.

§5.3 two-proof bar (Phase 4 §4.1 lock-in). (a) Caller-context: parseJoinedTable is a dedicated nonterminal function mirroring PG's joined_table production (gram.y:13554-13600). It is reached only from parseParenTableRef (select.go:1162) after the parenBeginsSubquery T3 snapshot scanner classifies the '('-led content as a joined_table (not a select_with_parens). Inside, a recursive paren handling is allowed (joined_table → '(' joined_table ')' in PG), and non-JoinExpr results are rejected as T7 post-parse validation — so the function cannot silently fall back to a subquery reading. (b) Empirical proof: paren_multi_join_test.go TestParseParenMultiJoin (lines 23-55, 11 pinned scenarios covering single/double/triple paren-wrapped joined_table, aliases, column-list aliases, subquery operands inside the outer parens, and the BYT-9315 pg_get_viewdef shape) + TestParseParenMultiJoinAST (lines 60-92, AST-shape left-associative JoinExpr verification) + TestParseParenTableRefRejectsNonJoined (lines 125-143, 3 rejection scenarios where inner content is not joined_table). Anti-regression fence: paren_oracle_from_joined_test.go TestParenOracleFromJoined drives the full PG 17 oracle probe for joined_table shapes; pgregress `join.sql` and `join_hash.sql` exercise production join syntax end-to-end.

Simple column list in SEARCH BY. No alternatives; '(' conditional on grammar production structure. Exercised by pgregress `with_recursive.sql` (SEARCH BREADTH / DEPTH FIRST BY col_list SET seq); unconditional paren shape.
Expect followed by parser.expect; no ambiguity. Exercised by pgregress `with_recursive.sql` CYCLE-clause scenarios; unconditional expect sequence.
Unconditional p.expect(')'). Exercised by pgregress `with_recursive.sql` CYCLE clause closing paren.
Another unconditional expect(')'). Exercised by pgregress `with_recursive.sql` CYCLE clause (USING path_col); unconditional expect.
Another unconditional expect(')'). Exercised by pgregress `with_recursive.sql` CYCLE closing paren variant.

Phase 1 §1.3: T1 peek on the first token after ARRAY — '[' routes to parseArrayExpr (array_expr constructor producing A_ArrayExpr), '(' routes to parseSelectStmtForExpr wrapped in SubLink{ARRAY_SUBLINK}. Other leads error. Caller-context: parseArrayCExpr is the sole consumer of the ARRAY c_expr lead (dispatched from c_expr at expr.go:1491 after the ARRAY keyword is matched); no other callers. Pinned scenarios (paren_array_expr_test.go TestParenArrayExprDispatch at line 37): constructor form (6) — ARRAY[1,2,3] line 43, ARRAY['a','b'] line 44, ARRAY[]::int[] line 47, ARRAY[] line 53, ARRAY[[1,2],[3,4]] line 58, ARRAY[1,2] || ARRAY[3] line 61; sublink form (4) — ARRAY(SELECT 1) line 64, ARRAY(SELECT x FROM t) line 65, ARRAY(SELECT DISTINCT ... ORDER BY) line 66, ARRAY(VALUES (1),(2)) line 67. AST-shape proof: TestParenArrayExprConstructorAST (line 87) asserts A_ArrayExpr.Elements len==2, TestParenArrayExprNestedConstructorAST (line 107) asserts nested A_ArrayExpr raw shape, TestParenArrayExprSublinkAST (line 224) asserts SubLink.SubLinkType==ARRAY_SUBLINK. Ref: gram.y:15440-15459 + 16583-16595.

Optional paren-wrapped precision. T1 peek sufficient: '(' signals precision variant, else no-arg form. Exercised by pgregress `date.sql`, `timestamp.sql`, `timestamptz.sql`, `time.sql` via SQL value functions (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(n), LOCALTIME(n), LOCALTIMESTAMP(n)).

After column ID: '(' signals func_call. Same as parseRelationExpr sites (already aligned C1). Exercised by pgregress `select.sql`, `functional_deps.sql`, `expressions.sql` — any unqualified function call; also double_precision_test.go TestParseCreateFunctionDoublePrecisionAllArgClasses.
After schema.attr: '(' signals func_call (qualified function). Exercised by pgregress `rangefuncs.sql`, `create_function_sql.sql` — schema-qualified function calls (pg_catalog.count etc.).
ROW keyword disambiguates; expect('(') after ROW is always explicit_row. Exercised by pgregress `rowtypes.sql`, `tuplestore.sql` — ROW(...) explicit row constructor; unconditional after ROW keyword.
Empty row check; conditional advance only. Exercised by pgregress `rowtypes.sql` — empty ROW() and ROW(a,b) forms; conditional advance for empty-row detection.
After EXTRACT; unconditional expect('('). No ambiguity. Exercised by pgregress `date.sql`, `timestamp.sql`, `interval.sql` — EXTRACT(field FROM value) sites; empty-paren guard.

Phase 1 §1.1: T8 fast path (isSelectStart on SELECT/VALUES/WITH/TABLE) commits to the subquery branch; when the post-'(' token is itself '(' the ambiguous case is resolved by lookaheadInIsSubquery — a T3 snapshot/restore scan tracking paren balance, where top-level ',' → expr_list, top-level UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT → subquery, and single-element falls through to a FIRST-set probe past leading '('. Completion-mode guard added at lookaheadInIsSubquery entry because snapshotTokenStream does not cover collecting/candidates state (backtrack.go:8-14); skipping the scan when p.completing is true preserves candidate registration integrity. Caller-context: parseInExpr is entered only from precedence climbing after IN / NOT IN token consumption (expr.go:264, 294); empty `IN ()` explicitly rejected. Pinned scenarios (paren_in_expr_test.go TestParenInExprDispatch at line 25): expr_list accept — IN (1,2,3) line 31, IN (a,b,c) line 32, IN ('a','b') line 33, IN (1) line 34; subquery accept — IN (SELECT y FROM u) line 36, IN (SELECT 1) line 37, IN (SELECT 1 UNION SELECT 2) line 38, IN ((SELECT 1) UNION (SELECT 2)) line 42, IN (VALUES ...) line 43, IN (WITH cte AS ... SELECT) line 44, IN (TABLE foo) line 45; empty reject — IN () line 49; row-LHS — (x,y) IN (SELECT a,b FROM u) line 51, (x,y) IN ((1,2),(3,4)) line 52; NOT IN variants — lines 57-60; partition_prune regression — IN ((SELECT 1),(SELECT 2)) line 68, IN ((SELECT 1)) line 72. AST-shape proof: TestParenInExprSubqueryAST (line 93, ANY_SUBLINK), TestParenInExprExprListAST (line 118, A_Expr AEXPR_IN with name '='), TestParenInExprNotInSubqueryAST (line 149, BoolExpr NOT wrap), TestParenInExprNotInExprListAST (line 179, operator '<>'). Ref: gram.y:14973-14998.
After IN; unconditional expect('('). Exercised by pgregress `subselect.sql`, `join.sql` — IN (SELECT ...) sublink; also paren_in_expr_test.go TestParenInExprSubqueryAST (ANY_SUBLINK assertion).

After '('; checks for empty parens or special forms (*, DISTINCT, ALL, VARIADIC). All branches handle ')' correctly. Exercised by pgregress `aggregates.sql` (DISTINCT/ALL/VARIADIC/*), `window.sql`, `select.sql` — all func_application variants; also paren_in_expr_test.go indirectly.

Inside brackets; array subscript or slice. Conditional parsing of two indices. Exercised by pgregress `arrays.sql`, `array.sql` — subscript and slice expressions; also paren_oracle_array_test.go TestParenOracleArray.

Phase 1 §1.4 (§5.3 two-proof bar). (a) Caller-context: parseArrayCExpr is the sole caller of this site; parseArrayCExpr itself is invoked exclusively from c_expr dispatch at expr.go:1491 after the ARRAY keyword is matched (gram.y:15440 c_expr: ARRAY select_with_parens / ARRAY array_expr). The '(' branch here is entered only after the preceding T1 peek at expr.go:1609 rules out '['. Inside, parseSelectStmtForExpr (= parseSelectNoParens) returns a typed-nil *SelectStmt when the paren content is not a select_no_parens lead; the T7 nil check type-asserts `*nodes.SelectStmt` and rejects when the boxed pointer is nil, preventing a downstream crash on SubLink{Subselect=(*SelectStmt)(nil)}. (b) Empirical proof (paren_array_expr_test.go TestParenArrayExprSubqueryContract at line 151, 6 pinned scenarios): accept — `SELECT ARRAY(TABLE foo)` (line 162-165, simple_select TABLE arm), `SELECT ARRAY(WITH cte AS (SELECT 1) SELECT * FROM cte)` (line 169-173, with_clause + select_clause), `SELECT ARRAY(VALUES (1), (2))` (line 175-179, simple_select VALUES); reject — `SELECT ARRAY(ROWS FROM (f(1)))` (line 186-190, ROWS FROM is func_table-only, not in select_no_parens), `SELECT ARRAY(1)` (line 194-198, bare expr is not a select_no_parens lead), `SELECT ARRAY()` (line 202-206, empty parens — pre-§1.4 this produced a SubLink{Subselect=nil}). Additional AST-shape proof: TestParenArrayExprSublinkAST (line 224) asserts SubLink.SubLinkType==ARRAY_SUBLINK for ARRAY(SELECT 1). Ref: gram.y:15440-15451.
